name: Container Load Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool for optimizing container utilization, reducing shipping costs, and minimizing waste per ISO and Lean standards.

inputs.label: Container Type
inputs.businessContext: Select the container type to define internal volume and dimensional constraints per ISO 668.

businessContext_i18n.en: Select the container type to define internal volume and dimensional constraints per ISO 668.

inputs.label: Cargo Length (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Length of the primary cargo unit (pallet or box). Must align with container internal length.

businessContext_i18n.en: Length of the primary cargo unit (pallet or box). Must align with container internal length.

inputs.label: Cargo Width (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Width of the cargo unit. Affects lateral stacking and aisle space.

businessContext_i18n.en: Width of the cargo unit. Affects lateral stacking and aisle space.

inputs.label: Cargo Height (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Height of the cargo unit. Must respect container door height and internal height.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height of the cargo unit. Must respect container door height and internal height.

inputs.label: Cargo Weight per Unit (kg)
inputs.businessContext: Weight per cargo unit. Critical for gross weight limit and axle load distribution.

businessContext_i18n.en: Weight per cargo unit. Critical for gross weight limit and axle load distribution.

inputs.label: Stacking Factor
inputs.businessContext: Number of cargo units that can be stacked vertically, considering crush resistance and stability.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of cargo units that can be stacked vertically, considering crush resistance and stability.

inputs.label: Load Securing Method
inputs.businessContext: Method used to secure cargo. Affects usable volume and weight distribution per CTU Code.

businessContext_i18n.en: Method used to secure cargo. Affects usable volume and weight distribution per CTU Code.

inputs.label: Use Pallet
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if cargo is palletized. Pallet dimensions add to base footprint and reduce usable height.

businessContext_i18n.en: Indicates if cargo is palletized. Pallet dimensions add to base footprint and reduce usable height.

inputs.label: Pallet Height (cm)
inputs.businessContext: Height of the pallet (if used). Added to cargo height for total unit height.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height of the pallet (if used). Added to cargo height for total unit height.

rules.condition: cargo_length_cm <= container_internal_length AND cargo_width_cm <= container_internal_width AND (cargo_height_cm + pallet_height_cm) <= container_internal_height
rules.message: Cargo dimensions exceed container internal dimensions. Adjust cargo size or select larger container.
Total cargo weight exceeds container maximum gross weight. Reduce units or weight per unit.
Stacking height exceeds container internal height. Reduce stacking factor.

thresholds.message: Volume utilization below 70%. Consider reconfiguring load or using smaller container.
Weight utilization exceeds 90%. Risk of overweight fines and unsafe handling.
Void space exceeds 15%. Use dunnage or air bags to prevent cargo shift.

formulas.name: Container Internal Dimensions
formulas.description: Retrieve internal length, width, height, and max gross weight based on container type per ISO 668.
formulas.formula: CASE container_type WHEN '20ft_standard' THEN {length: 589.8, width: 235.2, height: 239.2, max_gross_kg: 24000} WHEN '40ft_standard' THEN {length: 1203.2, width: 235.2, height: 239.2, max_gross_kg: 30480} WHEN '40ft_highcube' THEN {length: 1203.2, width: 235.2, height: 269.2, max_gross_kg: 30480} WHEN '20ft_open_top' THEN {length: 589.8, width: 235.2, height: 233.0, max_gross_kg: 24000} END

formulas.name: Total Unit Height
formulas.description: Calculate total height of one cargo unit including pallet if used.

formulas.name: Units per Layer
formulas.description: Maximum number of cargo units that can be placed on one layer based on floor area utilization (Lean: 85% target).

formulas.name: Total Layers
formulas.description: Number of vertical layers possible considering stacking factor and total unit height.

formulas.name: Total Units
formulas.description: Total number of cargo units that can be loaded into the container.

formulas.name: Volume Utilization
formulas.description: Ratio of cargo volume to container internal volume (Six Sigma metric for waste reduction).

formulas.name: Weight Utilization
formulas.description: Ratio of total cargo weight to container max gross weight.

primary.label: Total Loadable Units
primary.description: Maximum number of cargo units that can be loaded into the selected container.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: Unused volume due to dimensional mismatch or stacking constraints.
Unused weight capacity due to volume-limited loading.
Loss from inability to fully utilize stacking factor due to height constraints.

suggestedActions.description: Consider using half-pallets or removing pallets to increase unit count.
Increase stacking factor if cargo crush resistance allows.
Switch to a 40 ft High Cube if height is the limiting factor.
Use mixed cargo sizes to fill void spaces (Lean: reduce muda).
